title: cassipourea annobonensis
intro: Cassipourea annobonensis, also known as Annobon wood or Cassipourea, is a plant species native to the island of Annobón in the Gulf of Guinea. It is a medicinal plant with reported properties such as antimalarial and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Cassipourea annobonensis include its potential to treat fever, malaria, and other infections, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is used in herbal preparations, often in the form of teas or infusions, to leverage its medicinal properties.
